prompt: Painterly Elysian-Cathedral illustration, landscape 16:9, a volcanic cathedral interior. The nave is carved from black basalt and obsidian, with heavy obsidian columns veined with glowing lava-red cracks running down their lengths. Between the columns, narrow stained-glass windows in deep crimson, iron grey, and molten orange with Embertide-of-Power hints in the tracery, heavy black leading. A forge glow from deeper in the scene backlights the rear columns, casting a warm ember cast on the nearer basalt ridges. Upper-left directional cathedral light mixes with the ember undercast. The floor at lower-center is a dark obsidian flagstone stage with faint lava-crack inlays — an OPEN CLEAR STAGE, no creatures, no figures, no text, no UI, no banners — it reads as a battlefield the UI will sit on top of. The outer columns and ceiling are significantly darker than the stage center. Overall image is dimmed (interior with glow) — target average brightness about 32%. Palette: black basalt (#0c0a0a), obsidian (#1a1418), lava-crack red-orange (#c9421a and #e06a1a), iron gunmetal (#2a262a), ember highlight (#d47a1a). Soft anti-aliasing. No hard UI edges. No text, no letters, no watermark, no logos.
compositionNotes: Obsidian stage at lower-center reads as the battlefield — brighter than the periphery
Lava-vein cracks on columns and floor communicate the volcanic zone identity
No figures, text, or UI elements baked into the raster
Forge glow from the rear anchors the scene depth
notes: 2K landscape (2048x1152), target overall luminance 30-40% per PRD §D1.
Post-gamma adjusted (gamma 0.54) to lift average brightness from source into the 30-40% band.
If this raster is regenerated, re-run Playwright visual-regression with --update-snapshots.
